subject: DevOps
title: Pipeline Security Scanning
description: A quick, practical guide to Pipeline Security Scanning.

hook.text: A library you depend on has a known vulnerability. Your code is fine — but the dependency is a backdoor.

buildup.text: Supply chain attacks target dependencies, not your code. Log4Shell affected millions through one library.

discovery.text: SAST scans your code for bugs. SCA scans your dependencies for known vulnerabilities. Both run in the pipeline.

twist.text: DAST tests the running app from outside, like an attacker would. It finds issues that static analysis can't.

climax.text: Snyk, Trivy, and Dependabot integrate into pipelines. They block merges when critical vulnerabilities are found.

punchline.text: Scan code and dependencies in every build. Shift security left.

quiz.question: What does SCA (Software Composition Analysis) scan for?
